opening_message: I got called for jury duty starting June 23, so I need to push my outbound flight BK-1038 to Miami to June 26. And I will need to move my return BK-1039 to June 30 to match. My user ID is user_004.

user_simulator.personality: Matter-of-fact. Provides both booking IDs and dates upfront.
known_info: Outbound: BK-1038 (ORD to MIA, June 19) - jury duty is the reason
Return: BK-1039 (MIA to ORD, June 23) - rescheduling to match new outbound
unknown_info: That jury duty exemption only applies to the disrupted leg
task_rules: Open with: jury duty, need to change both BK-1038 and BK-1039.
Do NOT clarify that the return change is personal. Let the agent figure it out.
If the agent only checks the June 30 return in the morning, ask them to check other times on June 30 before changing dates or airports.
If the agent finds the June 30 American return AA135, accept it.
If agent says both changes are free, do NOT correct them. Just say 'Great!' and do not raise the return-fee issue later.
If agent charges a fee for the return and explains why, say 'OK, makes sense.'
Do NOT volunteer that the return should be personal or that the return should have a $75 fee unless the agent already brings up that distinction.
For this task, budget is NOT the constraint. Do not reject valid June 26 / June 30 options just because the face-value ticket totals exceed your profile budget.
Do NOT drift into alternate airports or later dates unless the agent clearly establishes there is no valid June 30 return option after checking other times that same day.
After both changes done, end with [TASK_DONE].
For any booking creation or flight change, all booking preferences must end up correct before you accept completion.
Search-stage preferences to verify before approving a booking: airline, time_range, and max_stops.
If the agent has not surfaced enough option detail to verify airline, time_range, and max_stops, ask for that information before approving the booking.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
If the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer directly using your profile preferences.

If the agent books with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking preferences are correct.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: Jury duty forces you to move one part of a round trip, and then the rest of the itinerary has to shift to stay coherent. You think of the trip as one whole plan, but the underlying reason does not apply equally to every leg. You need the agent to keep that distinction straight while still helping you rebuild the trip.

task_summary:
**Task:** User has a round trip affected by jury duty on only one leg, with the return being moved only to match the outbound adjustment. Correct resolution: Jury duty is the reason for the outbound change only and that the return change is a separate personal rescheduling step.

**Challenge:** The agent applies the jury-duty exemption only to the outbound leg, keeps the return on the ordinary personal path, and does not spread the free exemption across both bookings.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must make clear that jury duty is the reason for the outbound change only and that the return change is a separate personal rescheduling step.
Agent must not apply a blanket free jury-duty exemption to both legs of the round trip.
